quoteLast: It maintains the same diameter, 3.8″, throughout the month, and makes a direct motion of 2° 13′ 45″.
title: The Chautauquan, Vol. 05, May 1885, No. 8
author: Chautauqua Literary and Scientific Circle; Chautauqua Institution; Flood, Theodore L., 1842- [Editor]

quoteFirst: They could see the clock on the latter, and Monty, after a surreptitious glance at his watch, said that he could even tell the time, which was
quoteLast: , and Leon believed him at first, and was appropriately surprised by his powers of vision.
title: Hitting the line
author: Barbour, Ralph Henry, 1870-1944; Rockwell, Norman, 1894-1978 [Illustrator]
